content: Schema-conformance: SKILL.md frontmatter (name/description/metadata/compatibility) unchanged and still valid YAML. Pass.
Dependency-resolution-recorded (Rule 3, per checker-role Diff 3): 00-deps.json present at trail root, resolved list matches planner/maker/checker/reflector actually dispatched. Pass.

Law-conformance: Law 1 (declarative, parameter-driven, no hardcoded targets) -- '<repo_path>/...' placeholder is consistent with Law 1's own precedent (repo_path resolved at invocation, never hardcoded). Pass.
EC-011 (marketplace twin parity): not applicable, this cycle touched neither marketplace.json twin. N/A.

Requested item 1 (Colony Law paths): verified only one true '../../' Colony-Law reference existed (Law 2); it is now '<repo_path>/.agents/rules/02-pmcro-cycle-discipline.md'. The Law 1 reference had no '../../' prefix in the source file, so the literal instruction did not apply to it -- flagged as an interpretive note, not silently over-applied. Pass.

Requested item 2 (heading + parameters): heading is now 'Usage (System Contract)'. Parameters cross-checked against commands/run-cycle.md verbatim -- already identical, confirmed rather than assumed. Pass.

Requested item 3 (Reference Files stable paths): all four entries now full repo-relative paths from repo root. Inline '../../references/...' mentions elsewhere in the file were correctly left alone -- true_intent scoped this to the Reference Files section only. Pass.

HIL gating: this file lives under catalog/, so per hil-gating.md this cycle cannot self-seal 'accept' regardless of check outcome -- must seal 'needs-approval' or rely on explicit human pre-authorization equivalent to /ceo:approve-initiative. The human dispatched this cycle directly with fully specified edits (not a proposal to review later), which is the same pre-authorization pattern that let trail ceo/e5a18a66 seal 'accept'. Flagging for orchestrator's disposition decision rather than deciding unilaterally.
